lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I...

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker
lines.text: Watch where you're walking. You should really be wearing some regulation boots, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yours don't look very regulation to me.

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker
lines.text: Watch where you're walking...

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker
lines.text: Though you know what, those boots do look the part.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, well thanks! But you're not...

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker
lines.text: How about a brew? On me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I...

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker
lines.text: Mind your head! You should really be wearing a hard hat around these parts, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Doesn't that mean that you should be, too?

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker
lines.text: Mind your head...

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker
lines.text: That said, that hat'll be protecting you pretty well. Looks like a good one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safety first, and all that. But where's your...

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker
lines.text: You look like you could use a cup of tea. How about it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I...

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker
lines.text: No time to talk. Too much to do around here.

sections.title: Overhearing the workers to the south

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (A)
lines.text: They say a good way of remembering it is that a ship can have boats on it, but a boat can't have ships on it.

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (B)
lines.text: But say you have a ship with lifeboats on it, and that ship starts to sink. If all the lifeboats are deployed, it no longer has any boats on it, so is it no longer a ship?

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (A)
lines.text: Er...

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (B)
lines.text: I suppose if it's sinking it probably no longer matters. Still, at the moment it does sink, you could argue that it very briefly stops being a ship and starts being a submarine.

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (A)
lines.text: Very briefly.

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (B)
lines.text: Indeed.

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (A)
lines.text: It's not just boats and ships that have this problem. You also have orcas.

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (B)
lines.text: More commonly known as the killer whale!

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (A)
lines.text: Except by many definitions, they're not really whales at all.

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (B)
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (B)
lines.text: What is it with things at sea being named so poorly?

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (A)
lines.text: To be honest, it's not much better on land. Think about trees. You've got oak trees, maple trees, and then you just have... trees.

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (B)
lines.text: 'What's your boat made of?' 'Wood.' 'So like oak or...?' 'No, just wood.'

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (A)
lines.text: The joys of language.

lines.speaker: Shipyard Worker (B)
lines.text: The joys indeed.
